project: kcl/tregex — TRegEx-shaped regex API for bash over the [[ =~ ]] ERE engine (kklass static class)
status: PLANNED — plan + ledger only, no implementation yet. Roadmap position 2/7.
notes: Single source of truth for tregex status. Plan: kcl/tregex/PLAN.md. API spec: Delphi System.RegularExpressions.TRegEx (DocWiki; .NET Regex behavior breaks ties). ENGINE: bash [[ =~ ]] POSIX ERE + BASH_REMATCH — NOT PCRE; every dialect delta is documented and pinned by tests, never silently emulated. FPC packages/regexpr (TRegExpr) is reference-only, its engine is not ported. Workflow: phase -> dual-bash -> master sweep -> STOP -> 'go'; commits gated.

baseline.measured: 2026-07-12 — at roadmap planning time (post-tdictionary). RE-MEASURE at P0: roadmap unit 1 (tstopwatch) will have raised the totals by the time tregex starts.

suites_green.kcl_breakdown: tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155, dateutils 123, math 81, tdictionary 130

baseline.regression_rule: tregex work must keep the (re-measured) baseline green; the new tregex suite adds on top.

design_decisions.engine: bash [[ =~ ]] (POSIX ERE, C-speed, fork-free, internally cached compiled patterns). A bash-implemented engine would be ~100x slower and thousands of lines — rejected. The dialect delta vs PCRE is the documented price: no lazy quantifiers, no lookaround, no named groups, no pattern backrefs, POSIX classes instead of \d\w\s guarantees, leftmost-LONGEST alternation. Each delta gets a README table row AND a pinning test.
design_decisions.pattern_via_variable: Patterns are ALWAYS matched through a local variable (local __re="$2"; [[ $text =~ $__re ]]) — quoted RHS goes literal, inline (a|b) is a bash parse error (math P7 incident, pinned kcl idiom).
design_decisions.offsets: =~ gives no positions. Recovery: __pre=${text%%"${BASH_REMATCH[0]}"*}; RESULT_INDEX=${#__pre}. Exact for unanchored patterns (context-free substring matching); documented caveat for ^ $ \b anchored patterns (P0 probe matrix). Global scans iterate on the remainder with absolute-offset accounting.
design_decisions.flags: Single optional flags argument; v1 functional flag: 'i' (scoped nocasematch with fork-free save/restore on every return path). roMultiLine wontfix (no per-line anchors in =~); '.' matches newline by default (S1 probe).
design_decisions.global_scan: matches/replace/split loop on the remainder; EMPTY MATCH advances scan by one char (PCRE/.NET convention, pinned by test); maxCount optional bound on replace (bash extra).
design_decisions.replacement_grammar: $0..$9 groups, $$ literal dollar, unknown $x literal (DocWiki/.NET pin at P0). Pure-bash assembly from BASH_REMATCH — sed metachars (& \) are LITERAL in replacements (documented delta vs sed expectations).
design_decisions.errors: No match -> rc=1. INVALID PATTERN -> rc=2 (bash-native =~ signal, preserved and documented; debug msg under VERBOSE_KKLASS=debug).
design_decisions.static_class: No instances, no compiled-pattern objects: TRegEx.Create form is wontfix (bash caches compilation internally; an object adds dispatch cost and nothing else).

goals: TRegEx-shaped API (isMatch/match/matches/replace/split/escape + replaceCb extra) with RESULT/RESULT_INDEX/RESULT_LENGTH/RESULT_GROUPS contract and lossless nameref array fills.
Zero forks on every path incl. the i-flag save/restore; PATH='' tests.
The ERE-vs-PCRE delta table is part of the deliverable: every divergence documented AND pinned by a test (the delta is the spec).
Offset recovery exact for unanchored patterns, honestly-documented caveat for anchored ones (P0 probe matrix drives the wording).

test_basis.seed_primary: None (no FPC tests for this API). Basis: DocWiki TRegEx member examples, .NET Regex documented behavior (Split/Replace/empty-match rules), ERE spec (POSIX) for engine behavior.

parity_model.tier_A_exact: Everything the ERE engine supports: byte-exact matches, group contents, offsets (unanchored), replacement assembly, split pieces, escape output, rc codes. Divergences from PCRE are pinned AS divergence tests.

test_basis.new_coverage_protocol: All tests are non-FPC by construction -> every test gets a TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md row (basis: DocWiki/.NET/POSIX reading or probe result).

out_of_scope.item: Lazy quantifiers, lookaround, named groups, pattern backrefs, inline modifiers (?i)(?x), roExplicitCapture/roIgnorePatternSpace/roCompiled/roNotEmpty
out_of_scope.reason: Not in POSIX ERE; silent emulation would lie about the engine. Documented in the README delta table.
bash =~ has no multiline mode; split lines first (kcl has the tools).
ERE is leftmost-longest; pinned by an explicit divergence test.
Replaced by RESULT_* + nameref fills (kcl house style); object protocol is pure dispatch overhead; bash caches compiled patterns internally.

phases.name: Probe matrix + design pin + skeleton
phases.goal: Answer S1-S12 (PLAN section 3) on BOTH bashes; finalize offsets/flags/replacement grammar; skeleton + runner; baseline re-measure.

tasks.title: Probe matrix S1-S12 on 5.2.37 AND 5.3.9 (dot-newline, empty pattern, rc=2, nocasematch scope, offset recovery exact/anchored, \b availability, leftmost-longest, locale, unset groups, split semantics)
tasks.acceptance: Probe results recorded in this ledger; PLAN section 2/3 wording finalized from data, not assumptions.

tasks.title: Static class skeleton + tests runner + baseline re-measure (master suite before the runner exists)
tasks.acceptance: source tregex.sh green on both bashes; baseline recorded.

phases.name: isMatch / match / escape
phases.goal: Single-match core + RESULT contract + groups + escape.

tasks.title: isMatch/match with RESULT, RESULT_INDEX, RESULT_LENGTH, RESULT_GROUPS; escape() full metachar set; i-flag
tasks.acceptance: Torture subjects (newlines/globs/quotes/unicode/empty), offset adversarial set green, zero-fork PATH='', dual-bash, master sweep gate.

phases.name: matches / split
phases.goal: Global scan loop + empty-match rule + nameref fills.

tasks.title: matches (texts+offsets fills, RESULT=count) and split (S12 semantics: captures/empties per .NET pin)
tasks.acceptance: Empty-match advance-by-one pinned; overlapping-candidate cases; N-occurrence scaling sanity; dual-bash; sweep gate.

tasks.title: replace ($0..$9/$$ grammar, maxCount, replace-ALL default) + replaceCb (callback contract)
tasks.acceptance: Grammar edge cases (unknown $x, $$, groups beyond count), sed-metachar-literal pins, callback rc handling, dual-bash, sweep gate.

phases.name: Docs, bench, closeout
phases.goal: README + delta table, docs/TRegEx.md, bench, coverage notes, final sweep.

tasks.title: README.md (API + ERE-vs-PCRE delta table) + docs/TRegEx.md (upstream Delphi reference per kcl docs convention)
tasks.acceptance: Every public member + every wontfix delta documented.

tasks.title: bench.sh (dispatch vs raw =~, matches scaling, replace throughput), timed via TStopwatch.getTimeStamp (shared kcl bench clock — source ../tstopwatch/tstopwatch.sh; do NOT hand-roll _now_us) + TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES finalization + final sweep, ledger COMPLETE
tasks.acceptance: Numbers recorded; final master sweep green.

execution_log.summary: tregex PLAN.md + ledger written; folders created. No implementation. Load-bearing decision recorded: TRegEx API shape over the bash ERE engine with a documented-and-tested delta table (never silent emulation); pattern-via-variable idiom mandatory; offset recovery via prefix-strip with anchored-pattern caveat to be probed at P0.
